title: dendrophthoe neelgherrensis
intro: Dendrophthoe neelgherrensis, also known as the Indian mistletoe, is a hemiparasitic plant species that obtains its nutrients by parasitizing the host tree, usually the Neel (Melia azedarach). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Dendrophthoe neelgherrensis include reducing fever, treating respiratory infections, and alleviating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is used to prepare herbal remedies, including decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions.
